text.tspan: ARTIFACT 03H · QUESTION LIST
Every question, in order, grouped by section.
Step 0 is the only branching question. After triage, each visible section shows its full item set. Pain & Inflammation is the only three-state section (Yes / No / Not sure). The rest are binary
checks. Section text below is the exact UI string from the live assessment.
items across 5 sections
STEP 0 · TRIAGE · 5 SYMPTOM CHIPS
Where does it hurt? Pick all that apply, or "Show me everything."
Branches into 1–5 sections based on selection. "Not sure → show me everything" surfaces all 5.
Nails
Skin
Pain
Alignment
Footwear fit
Not sure → show me everything
SECTION 01 · BINARY CHECK (✓ / UNCHECKED)
Nail Health
Check both feet. Toenail problems are common and treatable. Most men don't know
what they're looking at.
Nails are thick, yellowed, white, or brittle
Nails crumble or break easily at the edges
One or more nails are lifting away from the nail bed
Any nail has dark spots or streaks under it
Big toenail curves into the skin at the sides
Nails are growing unevenly or in a curved shape
There is pain, redness, or discharge around any nail
NOTE
3+ checks likely points to fungal infection or an ingrown nail. Both are treatable.
SECTION 02 · BINARY CHECK (✓ / UNCHECKED)
Skin & Heels
Your heels have no oil glands. Cracking isn't a hygiene failure. It's biology. But it is fixable.
Heels have visible cracks or fissures (even shallow ones)
Skin on heels or balls of feet is hard, calloused, or rough
Skin peels between or around toes (athlete’s foot pattern)
There is redness, scaling, or itching between toes
Heels crack and bleed during dry months or after activity
Skin on the soles feels tight or pulls when flexing the foot
NOTE
2+ checks: a daily emollient routine after a shower solves most of this in 2 to 3 weeks.
SECTION 03 · THREE-STATE (YES / NO / NOT SURE)
Pain & Inflammation
Foot pain is information. Where it hurts tells you what is causing it.
Sharp heel pain with the first steps in the morning (then eases)
Burning or aching in the arch during or after standing
Ball-of-foot pain when walking or wearing dress shoes
Any joint in the foot is swollen, red, or warm to touch
Pain in the big toe joint, especially after rich food or alcohol
General aching in both feet by end of day
NOTE
Three-state because pain is interoceptive. Binary forces false positives. The only
section that asks for duration after the items.
SECTION 04 · BINARY CHECK (✓ / UNCHECKED)
Alignment & Structure
Your toes set the foundation. Misalignment doesn't stay in your feet. It travels up to
knees, hips, lower back.
Big toe leans toward the second toe (early bunion formation)
Lesser toes (2nd to 4th) curl downward when you relax your foot
You have corns or calluses on the tops of bent toes
You feel stiffness or limited movement in your big toe joint
Your feet turn outward when you walk (duck-footed gait)
NOTE
Alignment issues compound over years. Toe spreader exercises and footwear changes
early can slow or reverse drift.
SECTION 05 · BINARY CHECK (✓ / UNCHECKED)
Footwear Fit
The most upstream variable. Most foot problems trace back here.
Toes feel compressed when shoes are laced or buckled
Haven't had feet measured in the last 5 years
Shoe width pinches the widest part of your foot
Your heel slips out of the back when you walk
You wear the same size you've worn since your 20s
NOTE
Feet change shape and size as you age. If you have not been measured in a few years,
you probably are not in the right size.
PATTERN
Sections share four affordances.
Eyebrow with section number and answer-type tag. Title in Lora. Subtitle
explaining why this section exists. Numbered checklist of 5–7 items. Note at the
bottom that triages the flag count into a recommendation.
01 — Sections are introduced by symptom, not by anatomy.
02 — Items are first-person observations the user can verify in 5 seconds.
03 — Pain is the only three-state section. Pain answers are interoceptive.
04 — Flag count thresholds (1 / 2+ / 3+) gate the recommendation tier.
